title: Circumventing the Synthesizability Problem in Generative Molecular Design.
publicationName: Journal of chemical information and modeling
journalName: Journal of chemical information and modeling
snippet: Generative structure-based drug design (SBDD) models have shown great promise for accelerating our ability to discover novel drug candidates. However, these models have been criticized for producing compounds that are not very synthesizable and therefore not practically applicable to drug design. In this work, we propose a way to circumvent the synthesizabi...

title: The metabolic syndrome-cancer axis: global research trends and clinical landscapes.
snippet: The link between metabolic syndrome (MetS) and cancer has been widely studied, but overall research patterns, shared mechanisms, and applications remain unclear. A bibliometric and bioinformatics analysis is needed to identify trends and clarify the molecular pathways connecting MetS to cancer. title: Targeting BRD4 in gastric cancer: promoting apoptosis and suppressing tumor progression.
snippet: The MYC pathway is highly activated in gastric cancer (GC), but the MYC oncogene's structural biology makes direct pharmacological inhibition very difficult. We hypothesized that BRD4, as a major co-activator of MYC, maintains MYC-dependent survival circuits, thus making BRD4 a key therapeutic vulnerability in late GC. title: Fecal microbiome and metabolome dynamics during immunotherapy-based total neoadjuvant therapy in rectal cancer: associations with treatment response and toxicity.
snippet: Immunotherapy-based total neoadjuvant therapy (iTNT) is a promising strategy for microsatellite-stable locally advanced rectal cancer (LARC), yet therapeutic response and treatment-related toxicity remain heterogeneous. title: TNEAtlas: A Pan-cancer Database to Identify and Characterize Transcribed Non-coding Elements.
snippet: Advances in precision oncology have underscored the need to move beyond regulatory frameworks centered on protein-coding regions and better understand regulatory mechanisms within the non-coding genome. title: Integrated multi-omics analysis identifies prognostic risk genes and constructs a predictive signature in diffuse large B-cell lymphoma.
snippet: Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL) presents a complex etiology and challenging diagnosis. This study aims to investigate potential pathogenic genes. We identified DLBCL risk genes (DRGs) through expression quantitative trait loci-Mendelian randomization (eQTL-MR). title: Operationalizing microbiome ecology in cancer care.
snippet: Cancer treatment can disrupt the microbiome, worsening outcomes for cancer patients. title: SOPA and SIMPA: normalized single-sample integrated multiomics pathway analysis of tumor heterogeneity in solid cancers.
snippet: Inter-sample tumor heterogeneity poses significant challenges to metastatic cancer treatment. snippet: The co-occurrence of thyroid cancer (TC) and breast cancer (BC) has attracted much attention in recent years. title: Targeting the cytokine-epigenetic axis: a new paradigm and prospects for disease treatment.
snippet: Cytokines, as key signaling molecules, are involved in the regulation of physiological and pathological processes such as inflammation, immunity, and cell growth. title: Neutrophil extracellular traps drive cholangiocyte injury via ZNF709-TP53-MDM2 pathway in primary biliary cholangitis.
snippet: Primary biliary cholangitis (PBC) is a chronic autoimmune liver disease in which many patients respond inadequately to ursodeoxycholic acid (UDCA) and may progress to liver failure. title: Pulmonary mRNA-LNP vaccines for rapid and durable protection against bacterial infection.
